UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores application-specific user data, linked to an external authentication system. Does not contain sensitive authentication credentials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

externalAuthId.description: Identifier from the external authentication system, linking this profile to an authenticated user.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The primary email address of the user.

companyName.description: The name of the company the user is associated with, relevant for B2B solutions.

preferredTopicIds.description: References to topics of interest for content recommendations. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Topic)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Solution.title: Solution
Solution.description: Represents a specific digitalization, automation, or smart solution offered by A S Technosystems.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Solution entity.

title.description: The name or title of the solution.

shortDescription.description: A concise summary of the solution.

longDescription.description: A detailed explanation of the solution's features and functionality.

benefits.description: A list of key benefits provided by the solution.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image visually representing the solution.

iconUrl.description: URL to an icon representing the solution.

topicIds.description: References to topics associated with this solution, used for categorization and recommendations. (Relationship: Solution N:N Topic)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the solution was created in the system.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the solution was last updated.

Topic.title: Topic
Topic.description: Represents a category or area of interest, used to tag and organize solutions and content for recommendations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Topic entity.

name.description: The name of the topic (e.g., 'Digitalization', 'AI', 'Cloud Solutions').

description.description: A brief description of what the topic covers.

imageUrl.description: URL to an optional image associated with the topic.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the topic was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the topic was last updated.

ClientStory.title: ClientStory
ClientStory.description: Details a successful implementation of A S Technosystems' solutions for a client, including testimonials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ClientStory entity.

clientName.description: The name of the client featured in the success story.

industry.description: The industry the client operates in.

problemStatement.description: A description of the challenge or problem the client faced.

solutionImplementedIds.description: References to the solutions implemented for this client. (Relationship: ClientStory N:N Solution)

resultsAchieved.description: A description of the positive outcomes and results achieved for the client.

testimonialQuote.description: A direct quote from the client providing a testimonial.

clientLogoUrl.description: URL to the client's company logo.

caseStudyUrl.description: URL to a detailed case study document (e.g., PDF).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the client story was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the client story was last updated.

Inquiry.title: Inquiry
Inquiry.description: Stores details of inquiries submitted through the contact form.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Inquiry entity.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile if the inquiry was submitted by a logged-in user. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Inquiry)

fullName.description: The full name of the person submitting the inquiry.

email.description: The email address for responding to the inquiry.

companyName.description: The name of the company the inquirer represents.

subject.description: The subject line or topic of the inquiry.

message.description: The detailed message or request from the inquirer.

status.description: The current processing status of the inquiry (e.g., 'New', 'In Progress', 'Resolved').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the inquiry was submitted.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the inquiry was last updated (e.g., status change).

definition.description: Stores user profile data. The `userProfileId` matches `request.auth.uid` (and the `externalAuthId` field within the document) for direct path-based authorization. Only the owner can read/write, with admin override. Includes `createdAt` and `updatedAt`.

Inquiries submitted by authenticated users. The `userProfileId` in the path matches `request.auth.uid` for path-based authorization, and the `userProfileId` field within the document will be populated. Owner can create/read/update, with admin override.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique ID of the user profile, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: inquiryId
params.description: The unique ID of the specific inquiry.

definition.description: Publicly accessible information about A S Technosystems' solutions. Read-only for all users, write/update/delete restricted to administrators. Includes denormalized `topicIds` for categorization.

params.name: solutionId
params.description: The unique ID of the solution.

definition.description: Publicly accessible categories or areas of interest for solutions and content. Read-only for all users, write/update/delete restricted to administrators.

params.name: topicId
params.description: The unique ID of the topic.

definition.description: Publicly accessible details of client success stories and testimonials. Read-only for all users, write/update/delete restricted to administrators. Includes denormalized `solutionImplementedIds`.

params.name: clientStoryId
params.description: The unique ID of the client story.

definition.description: Inquiries submitted by anonymous users. The `userProfileId` field will be null or omitted. Allows creation by anyone, but read/list access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: inquiryId
params.description: The unique ID of the specific inquiry.

definition.description: Designates users with administrative privileges. Document existence indicates an admin role, with `adminId` matching `request.auth.uid`. Used for DBAC (Database-Backed Access Control) via `exists()` checks in security rules.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for AST Digital Hub prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable access control by strictly adhering to the core design principles. The strategy focuses on **Authorization Independence via Denormalization**, **Structural Segregation**, and **Path-Based Access Modeling**.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
1.  **User Profiles:** The `UserProfile` documents are stored at `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}`, where `{userProfileId}` is directly set to the `request.auth.uid` (which corresponds to the `externalAuthId` from the schema). This directly links the authenticated user's ID to their profile document ID. Any security rule for a user's profile can thus directly compare `request.auth.uid` with `userProfileId` in the path, eliminating the need for `get()` calls to check ownership.
2.  **Authenticated Inquiries:** Inquiries submitted by logged-in users are stored as a subcollection under their respective user profiles: `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/inquiries/{inquiryId}`. Here, the `{userProfileId}` in the path is again `request.auth.uid`, and the `userProfileId` field within the `Inquiry` document (if populated) will match this. This design ensures that rules for user-specific inquiries can rely solely on the path parameters and `request.auth.uid`, avoiding cross-document `get()` operations for authorization.
3.  **Public Collections:** Collections like `/solutions`, `/topics`, and `/clientStories` hold data that is globally readable. Since there are no user-specific authorization dependencies for general read access, no denormalization for authorization is required within these documents. Admin write access is managed via a separate, explicit admin role check.

**QAPs (Query as a Principal) Support:**
1.  **Public Data (`/solutions`, `/topics`, `/clientStories`):** These collections are designed for broad public access. Security rules will simply allow `list` and `read` operations for all users (`allow read: if true;`), ensuring that any query will securely return all accessible documents without filtering issues.
2.  **User-Owned Data (`/userProfiles/{userProfileId}`, `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/inquiries`):** For these collections, `list` operations are secured by matching the path's `userProfileId` to `request.auth.uid`. For example, a query on `/userProfiles/{request.auth.uid}/inquiries` will only return inquiries owned by the currently authenticated user. This intrinsically filters results at the security rule layer, guaranteeing all returned documents are authorized for the requesting user.
3.  **Anonymous Inquiries (`/anonymousInquiries`):** This collection is designed for `create` access by any user (authenticated or anonymous) but restricted `read` and `list` access primarily to administrators. This prevents anonymous users from listing or retrieving other submissions, maintaining data privacy while still allowing form submissions.

**Structural Segregation:**
*   Publicly accessible, static data (`solutions`, `topics`, `clientStories`) are in top-level collections.
*   User-specific data (`userProfiles`) is isolated under `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}`.
*   Authenticated user inquiries are nested under their respective user profiles (`/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/inquiries`).
*   Anonymous inquiries are in a separate top-level collection (`/anonymousInquiries`) to clearly separate their unique security posture (create-only for public, admin-read).
*   Administrative roles are in their own collection (`/roles_admin`) for explicit DBAC checks.

**Access Modeling & Predictability:**
*   **Path-Based Ownership:** Heavily utilized for `UserProfile` and user-specific `Inquiry` data.
*   **Global Roles (DBAC):** Implemented using `/roles_admin/{adminId}` where existence of the document signals admin privileges, facilitating simple and atomic rule checks without `get()` calls.
*   **Explicit State:** The `status` field in the `Inquiry` entity models finite states clearly, enhancing debuggability and predictability.
*   **Radical Consistency:** Wildcard parameters are descriptive (e.g., `{userProfileId}`, `{solutionId}`). `externalAuthId` is consistently used as the document ID for user profiles.
